title: Staleness/refresh: OER drift + standards re-publication detection
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. The crosswalk decays as OER move and frameworks re-publish; this keeps records and identifiers honest over time.
objective: Build staleness/refresh detection for OER drift and standards re-publication, regenerating the coverage ledger and re-releasing.
acceptanceCriteria: Link-check detects OER that moved/changed (404 / content drift vs. the recorded version+snapshot).
Detects standards framework re-publication (CASE version bump / changed identifiers); affected records flagged for possible re-review as maintenance tasks.
Coverage ledger regenerated; dataset re-released with bumped semver + date.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
